- Purple wall: rows 21-41, cols 33-37
The purple wall blocks movement between the left and right sides. The N(W) token is on the left side, and the N(B) token is on the right side (between the purple wall and the white square).
Wait, actually looking at the positions:
- N(W) at cols 18-20 is on the left panel
- N(B) at cols 27-29 is between the left panel and the purple wall (cols 33-37)
- White square at cols 30-32 is also between the left panel and the purple wall
Hmm, but the left panel is white (id=1) and spans cols 9-23. So:
- N(W) at cols 18-20 is inside the left panel
- N(B) at cols 27-29 is outside the left panel (between panels)
- White square at cols 30-32 is also between panels
The right panel is at cols 39-53. The right container is at cols 44-48, inside the right panel.
So the layout is:
Left panel (white, cols 9-23) → gap (cols 24-32) → Purple wall (cols 33-37) → Right panel (white, cols 39-53)
N(W) is in the left panel, N(B) and white square are in the gap between panels.
Wait, but the purple wall is at cols 33-37, and the N(B) is at cols 27-29 and white at cols 30-32. These are all to the left of the purple wall.
Let me re-examine. The left panel boundary is [[21, 9], [21, 23], ...]. So it spans rows 21-41 and cols 9-23 (approximately). The right panel is at cols 39-53.
So:
- Left panel: cols 9-23
- Gap: cols 24-32
- Purple wall: cols 33-37
- Right panel: cols 39-53
N(W) at cols 18-20 is inside the left panel.
N(B) at cols 27-29 is in the gap.
White square at cols 30-32 is in the gap, adjacent to the purple wall.
The left container is at cols 11-15, inside the left panel.
The right container is at cols 44-48, inside the right panel.
So the goal might be:
1. Move N(W) into the left container (cols 11-15)
2. Move N(B) into the right container (cols 44-48)
But the purple wall blocks access to the right panel from the left. How would N(B) get to the right container?
Wait, maybe the white square at cols 30-32 is the player avatar, and the N tokens are objects to be moved. Let me try moving the white square.
Actually, I notice that when I pressed RIGHT, the N(W) token moved. So directional controls affect the N tokens. But which one? It seems like RIGHT moved the leftmost N token (N(W)) to the right.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe:
- The N tokens are "players" or "agents"

- The goal is to get them into the containers
- The white squares are "bridges" or "tiles" that appear when N tokens move
